subject: A serpentine, many-armed wind kami coiled within a swirling vortex of air currents — pale blue and white trailing wraps, an elongated draconic head, multiple flailing arms streaming outward as if caught in its own gale
description: A massive serpentine spirit fills the frame, its body coiled into a tight knot at center while a vortex of pale wind whips around it. Multiple long, thin arms with grasping hands radiate outward in different directions, each trailing pale ribbons or sleeves of cloth. The head is elongated and draconic, with a pointed snout and small eyes, set high in the composition. The palette is dominated by chalky whites, soft sky-blues, and dust-greys, with the kami's body rendered in slightly warmer cream and tan tones. The background is an abstract storm of swirling currents — no solid ground, no horizon, just air. The whole image reads as motion and weightlessness, a deity made of wind and limbs.
foreground: coiled multi-armed serpentine kami with trailing wraps
background: swirling vortex of pale wind currents, no horizon
emotion: serene power, otherworldly stillness at the center of motion
